pages.raw_text:
BUTTE, Mont, April 27.-Thirty-
five buffalo were yesterday loaded at
Arlee for shipment to Oklahoma, and
they will go to Bliss, 900 miles south
of Kansas City, where an effort will
be made to perpetuate the species.
The animals were all adults, and were
the best specimens Of the American
bison that have even been shipped
from the reservation.


The animals were purchased by
George w. Miller who has been on
the reserve for the past week or
10 days, and considerable difficulty
was experienced in getting them into
the shipping pens. They are all from
the famous Michael Pablo herd, which
falo. and is the largest herd on
earth.


The deal was made through Major
Smead and the animals were shipped
through town last night in two palace
stock cars.


In well informed circles this ship
ment is taken as an indication that
the big buffalo herd on the reserva-
tion will be disbanded within the next
few years. and that the Indians on
the reserve will sell off every indi-
vidual in the big herd. the half blopds
as well as the full bloods.


With the opening of the reserva-
tion the grazing area will be limited,
and the buffalo will no longer have
the range facilities as afforded today
Consequently it will be necessary to
find some disposition for the rare ami
mails, for they will not range in a
section where the white men frequent
to any extent


The consideration of the deal has
not been made public, but 1t Is stated
that Mr.. Pablo secured a most sub
stantial figure for his Indian cattle"
